name: shodan-reconnaissance
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to \"search for exposed devices on the internet,\" \"perform Shodan reconnaissance,\" \"find vulnerable services using Shodan,\" \"scan IP ranges...
capabilities: Query Shodan for exposed devices and services using CLI and API
Search with filters to find specific products, vulnerabilities, and network ranges
Perform host reconnaissance to identify open ports and service banners
Use on-demand scanning to discover current states of target networks
Extract and analyze SSL certificate information
Generate vulnerability reports using CVE search filters
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 138 potential issues, but manual evaluation confirms all are false positives. The skill teaches legitimate use of Shodan, a recognized security research tool. Code examples show bash commands (not Ruby execution), placeholder API keys (not real secrets), and public/placeholder IP addresses. Includes explicit authorization requirements. Risk level set to LOW.
valueStatement: Identify vulnerable services and exposed devices on the internet using Shodan's comprehensive reconnaissance capabilities for penetration testing and security research.
limitations: Requires valid Shodan API key (free or paid account)
Rate limited to 1 request per second
Cannot re-scan same IP within 24 hours on non-Enterprise plans
Free accounts have limited query and scan credits

useCases.title: Penetration Testing Reconnaissance
useCases.description: Use Shodan to discover exposed services and vulnerabilities in target infrastructure during authorized security assessments.

useCases.title: Organization Asset Discovery
useCases.description: Map public-facing assets belonging to target organizations for attack surface analysis.

useCases.title: IoT and Vulnerability Monitoring
useCases.description: Find exposed IoT devices and services vulnerable to known CVEs across the internet.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Host Lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the shodan-reconnaissance skill to find information about IP address 1.1.1.1. What services are running and what country is it located in?

promptTemplates.title: Vulnerable Service Discovery
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all hosts in the US running vulnerable versions of Log4j (CVE-2021-44228) using Shodan search filters.

promptTemplates.title: Organization Reconnaissance
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform reconnaissance on an organization. Search for all hosts belonging to Google and get statistics on their infrastructure including ports and products.

promptTemplates.title: SSL Certificate Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Find hosts with SSL certificates for example.com domain. Show how to search for expired or self-signed certificates.

bestPractices: Always obtain written authorization before scanning target networks
Use Shodan credits efficiently by starting with free count queries
Combine multiple search filters to narrow results and find specific vulnerabilities
antiPatterns: Using Shodan to target systems without authorization is illegal and unethical
Do not rely solely on Shodan data for vulnerability assessment - verify findings manually
Avoid excessive scanning that may trigger rate limits or account suspension

faq.question: Is Shodan legal to use?
faq.answer: Shodan is a legal security research tool. However, using it to scan or target systems without authorization may violate laws. Always obtain written permission before investigating specific targets.

faq.question: Do I need a paid Shodan account?
faq.answer: A free account provides limited credits. Paid plans offer more queries and scan credits. Basic searches without filters are free.

faq.question: Can this skill help me hack systems?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides reconnaissance methodology for authorized security testing. Unauthorized access to computer systems is illegal.

faq.question: How accurate is Shodan data?
faq.answer: Shodan data may be days or weeks old. On-demand scans provide current data. Always verify critical findings with direct testing.

faq.question: What are Shodan credits?
faq.answer: Credits are consumed for filtered searches, downloads, and on-demand scans. Basic searches without filters are free. Check your balance with 'shodan info'.

faq.question: Can I scan private IP ranges?
faq.answer: Shodan crawls public internet. Private IP ranges (RFC 1918) like 192.168.x.x are placeholders in examples. Only public IPs can be scanned.
